[ndcur @ 2022-08-25]: How to best prepare for an Apple TPM Interview loop? While I know how to prepare for Meta, Google and Amazon, I'm not sure yet what's the framework that helps in making an Apple interview loop successful. Any hints?

[margie9000 @ 2022-08-27]: Should I apply for a TL role or a TPM role?

[artistic_yeoman @ 2022-03-31]: Hi all. I have a 1 hour interview with another TPM coming up for Amazon loop. from reading through messages on here, it seems like this is all behavior and systems design doesn't happen until onsite. I am wondering if I should spend the time to prep systems design for 1 hour Chime call or just focus on behavioral prep. If anyone has had to do systems design before onsite interview let me know. thanks!
  > [kyh_0824 @ 2022-03-31]: def take a look at #amazon
  > [be

[prac_tpm @ 2022-09-16]: Anyone gone through Expedia TPM interview process? How is the process and what is covered in the loop interview? The recruiter hasn't replied me with any prep material. Seems like they have no interview structure. If anyone has recently gone through the process, it would be really helpful!
[ellsmv5742 @ 2022-09-22]: Has anyone done a Meta EPM loop? I have one coming up and there’s a “technical presentation” component. Haven’t heard of this one before.
[hari3479 @ 2022-09

[t2020 @ 2023-10-04]: Completed a TPM loop at Amazon, 1 systems design (half time on behavior questions, one design problem), 4 leadership principles deep dive (~3-4 questions per interviewer). Very technical. My recruiter offered interview prep before the loop, it was helpful.

  > [t2020 @ 2023-10-12]: I did not get it. They did not offer any feedback.
  > [sunshinega

[ad1205. @ 2022-02-24]: Hi All, Sorry for the long post. Below is my experience with Amazon SR TPM interview. Hope this helps • 1st round with engineering director: What is the project that your most proud of and why. Asked follow-up questions on application architecture. Any experience with conflict management, how did you handle and what did you learn, provide examples. • I texted the recruiter 4 days after the asking for results and she told me I got selected for loop. Had 3 weeks’ time to pr

[margie9000 @ 2022-08-27]: Hi, I am a tech lead who does technical program management as well 60% of the time as my current role. I am running 2 programs with 30 engineers. I would like to grow as a TPM. I did the entire loop at Amazon for a Sr. TPM role. But I failed to convince them that I can do the job. Any suggestions?

[theguru7312 @ 2021-10-02]: Hello- Would anyone know the interview process for Amazon Manager, TPM role? Would there be System Design rounds for Onsite? Thanks!
  > [padmaddy @ 2021-10-03]: You could expect System design round and can also check with HR. Typically there should be one

[arav1nd @ 2021-07-29]: system design is more infra focused.
  > [alsworld1 @ 2021-07-29]: Is there system design in the phone screen or onsite?
  > [arav1nd @ 2021-07-29]: do you have just 1 phone screen or two? they had started experimenting with splitting out behavioral and tech screens into 2 separate 30min convos. idk if it was rolled out widely yet

[letsdoit021 @ 2022-05-05]: Will appreciate if anyone has recent experience
[Deleted User @ 2022-05-06]: OCI TPM doesn’t have separate systems design round, it’s more PgM and behavioral rounds
[googling7419 @ 2022-05-06]: Had my phone screen yesterday in afternoon with Meta but haven’t heard back from recruiter yet. Is it a reject? What do you peeps think as per your experience?

[chughes13x @ 2024-09-12]: Does anyone know if Cruise holds system design rounds for its TPM onsites? I've passed the first round and am in the process of scheduling my 5hr full loop.
  > [scalability1 @ 2024-09-25]: yes they do
  > [sarah019722 @ 2024-09-26]: Hi @scalability1 do you have any advice on the types of questions asked in system design interview / ways to prepare?
  > [scalability1 @ 2024-10-03]: it will be around their area. automated car system or air traffic system etc.

[prac_tpm @ 2022-09-06]: Anyone gone through a Privacy TPM technical phone screen round recently. I have one next week and wanted to know what I might be asked.
  > [jdizzle9388 @ 2022-09-07]: 3-4 LPs and questions on the projects you have listed on your resume.
  > [prac_tpm @ 2022-09-07]: What about system design?
  > [jdizzle9388 @ 2022-09-07]: Nope
  > [jdizzle9388 @ 2022-09-15]: How was your phone screen?
  > [prac_tpm @ 2022-09-15]: I got COVID. Had to reschedule it next week.

[golden_tiger @ 2022-09-05]: look up other systems design interviews because you'll get questions and push back along the way

[topnotch2199 @ 2021-12-19]: Hi Folks, reaching out this community for any of your inputs/advices. I am scheduled for a Product TPM Screen interview with the hiring manager at Meta and wondering what to expect in this round. Can this be more of any system/product design or technical project retrospective. Any guidance is much much appreciated ??
  > [tht2023 @ 2021-12-19]: A little bit of everything== a combo of all 5 of the on site loop interviews. The composition can vary depending on the team

[dmodemd @ 2021-12-31]: Infra TPM is what I landed but I haven't heard it described like that. Phone screen will be on the more generic behavioral TPM subjects. Later, sys design interview will be trying to get you to deep dive on building out a highly scalable, reliable, and performant infrastructure so will need to prep well for that.
  > [.lostping @ 2022-01-01]: Thanks. Is your role part of core infra ? How much prep did you do for initial phone screen?
  > [dmodemd @ 2022-01-01]: Yes and no

[arav1nd @ 2022-03-03]: I'm now confused :) There's no product sense interview for a TPM. That's your typical system design interview. For PMs there's product sense and product execution and that's a very different loop than SD for TPMs
  > [aaaabbbb7360 @ 2022-03-03]: thanks for clarifying

[tpminginla @ 2024-07-24]: hey guys, how would you answer questions around how you've driven organizational change?
[jayama @ 2024-07-24]: IMO, the best stories for organizational change are when you introduce or improve a process or system to uplevel the org that lives on after you stop actively driving it. (i.e. you're influencing the org as a whole as opposed to just carrying the load as a one-TPM army) The most common examples are systems for cross-team planning or driving campaigns/migratio

[vpanda123 @ 2024-06-08]: for a leadership role at one of the company, I was asked explain "In a team where TPM don't have direct authority over everyone, how do you assess a team member's ability to influence others and drive positive change at staff, Sr staff and principal levels".
  > [arav1nd @ 2024-06-08]: 1. peer feedback; what levels are they influencing and what do the folks in these levels tell you? I'd expect a Staff TPM to influence at Group PM and Sr EM levels, a Sr. Staff to influen

[tpm001 @ 2021-10-15]: Behavioral was pretty much standard questions and follow ups based on your response. Make sure to have examples focusing on high impact, leadership visibility, dealing with ambiguity, resolving conflicts and driving large programs.
[tpm001 @ 2021-10-15]: Good luck to anyone scheduled for any interviews ??
[arav1nd @ 2021-10-15]: I don't think FB interviewers expect folks to have worked on large scale systems. the expectation is to have understood the system, choices, tradeo

[adventurous_sparrow_89748 @ 2022-02-15]: What is everyone's preferred software for project management and why do you choose it over others?
  > [.eth1m @ 2022-02-15]: My approach is 1) find the common PM tool that your stakeholders are using 2) Understand the team use cases (get feedback) 3) define whether to use that tool or implement a new PM tool that solves the issues while taking into consideration on-ramp up etc. To answer your question - I have used most tools and the one I use the most

[ntc10 @ 2024-02-13]: I think TPM value ranges everywhere from unnecessary overhead to absolutely critical for landing big projects. The problem I think is multi-fold: 1.) companies don't have the right organizational culture / empowerment to effectively leverage TPMs. TPMs need to have a seat at the table and really be able to make program decisions. [ultra.instinct_ @ 2021-12-29]: That’s what i do. For stuff where i need to get shit done in excel
[dmodemd @ 2021-12-29]: I am so used to having to use what everyone else is.... (20 yrs consulting)
[dmodemd @ 2022-01-08]: TPM should be focused on the execution and engineering. PM will work on the requirements, marketing, customer engagement, documentation comsolidation, and other things. But each company is different in the division of responsibilities.

[arav1nd @ 2021-07-29]: This randomly showed up on my Google news feed. [link]
  > [bahvb7446 @ 2021-07-29]: Is it true Microsoft TPMs are a bit different than other company TPMs? I’ve heard they combine Project + product in one role (which that articles confirms) whereas TPM at other companies has those two separated.
  > [newname4167 @ 2021-07-29]: Correct
  > [borderline88 @ 2021-07-29]: Yes. MSFT PMs have to take care of a lot of things coz they never have enough engineering resources, so PM

[ntc10 @ 2024-02-13]: I think TPM value ranges everywhere from unnecessary overhead to absolutely critical for landing big projects. The problem I think is multi-fold: 1.) companies don't have the right organizational culture / empowerment to effectively leverage TPMs. TPMs need to have a seat at the table and really be able to make program decisions. There are also some things where you just don't need a TPM (e.g. project managing a small team) that should really be done by EMs/TLs and the comp

[happyfly9528 @ 2021-10-26]: How would you answer this : what's something you want to change in the TPM role?
  > [caliman9853 @ 2021-10-26]: I wouldn't answer this as a generic question. Every company has different expectations from TPMs.
  > [ihavenochance. @ 2021-10-26]: I think asking for at least 30-50% weightage in individual's annual performance rating would be a fair ask
  > [pkp2043 @ 2021-10-27]: In my personal experience this is company/culture specific. I used to work for Government

[scalability1 @ 2022-02-16]: TPMs also focus on why, what and how questions and depending on the company they can even totally own those or they may support those questions. This picture undervalues TPMs as only meeting coordinator with other teams.
  > [vkrish7 @ 2022-02-16]: Yeah. That’s because in some companies and orgs they act as meeting coordinators and meeting minute takers and that’s how the rest of the teams envision the role of TPM.

[Thread: Do You still Code?]
[rrvaluecreator @ 2025-09-04]: In my case, I started out as a Java Developer about two decades ago. These days, I still keep in touch with coding - mostly for structuring data, pulling data from APIs for analysis, small automations, or integrations. With the rise of AI, I find it amazing that I can now write programs in different languages with much more ease, and even understand or adapt scripts quickly. I’m curious - what about you? Do you still code in your TPM ro

[shak7921 @ 2022-02-23]: Just had an interesting call with a recruiter from Compass. He mentioned that they expect TPMs to code (i.e scripting) during their interview process. Anyone had their compass interview and had to go through this?
  > [vb2701 @ 2022-02-23]: Yes they have a coding take home assignment

[jayama @ 2025-03-02]: A traditional coding example: I'd say a TPM needs to understand the high level of what multi threading is and its implications (e.g. faster processing at the cost of higher complexity can lead to tricky issues that are hard to debug). But I would never say a TPM needs hands on coding experience working with mutexes and semaphores. That seems unnecessary to me.
  > [hackiebuterin @ 2025-03-08]: I don’t think that’s the ask

[arav1nd @ 2021-09-14]: Eng manager writes code
  > [mkuser. @ 2021-09-14]: Do they get paid higher since they code and manage team
  > [caliman9853 @ 2021-09-14]: FB EM code? At Amazon they're too busy with reporting and documenting that they'd never find the time needed to write code. I guess that's why TPMs can easily cross to SDMs at Amazon
  > [arav1nd @ 2021-09-14]: They get paid significantly more than TPM.

[hamjoy @ 2022-03-28]: Depends on the teams needs honestly. I have heard of some TPMs running them, but I've also heard of some TPMs learning coding to help unblock minor issues
  > [benbeinglass @ 2022-03-28]: Thank you. Appreciate your inputs.

I came across a startup in an industry that I'm passionate about. They are hiring for a product manager and have a small engineering team. Both to employee prospects and investors (financial trouble, negative growth). 2. Very hard to do hiring again later pre-internet. 3. Their unemployment tax rate they pay on everyone would spike. Now they make so much and much of the

We often had to check it when speaking with people in planning discussions so we had some context on if they were sticking around. There was a select group of employees that we brought "under the tent" via an NDA to help us plan the company post-layoffs they didn't know was comi

[dmodemd @ 2022-12-01]: It seems then he can sell stock for cash while not affecting his control. I think the layoff was more about getting rid of "coasters". We at Meta are slowly getting headcount approved and are getting some people back that were tagged as rehireable but were in groups being deprioritized.

Some bean counters, with VPs high above made the decisions and even D2s didn't know who or have a say. I heard in the Meta layoffs that even VPs were just given 1 or 2 "saves" to use.

[dmodemd @ 2021-12-30]: Amazon offers have the advantage of being fixed RSUs in the offer letter so you are already locked in when you see it and are making (or losing) money before you start!

[jayama @ 2024-08-30]: The semi-Gotcha: Amazon assumes 15% YoY stock growth of all RSUs they grant you. So if they grant you 1000 shares that would vest in 2025 when the stock is currently $200, they're not assuming that's 1000 x $200 ($200,000). They're forecasting that as 1000 x $200 *1.15 ($230,000) This made sense when the stock was crazy good, but less sense now. This is why Amazon has started providing higher portions of compensation in base salary instead of RSUs. Previously base salary w
